data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the OWNER of parent AC4 ("Sub-op telemetry (links/comments/labels applied) is surfaced, and a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` condition fails loudly") and parent SC3 ("A silent sub-op no-op … fails a test/canary instead of passing green"). Both FULLY covered: 2359 AC1 surfaces links/comments/labels_applied in batch outcome + RECON line (AC4 clause 1); 2359 AC2 tests computed>0 && applied==0 failure reproducing the link drop (AC4 clause 2 + SC3); AC3 keeps sync semantics unchanged + suite green (contributes AC7); AC4 asserts count invariant not log strings (contributes SC5/AC7). Criteria this child does not own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC1/AC2/SC1→8937+fe57+59ef; AC3/SC2→33d0; AC5/SC4→822a. Declared OUT items (probe wiring→822a; applier consolidation→33d0) consistent with roster. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 2359.

4-bucket for this pairing: AC4 + SC3 FULLY (this child); SC5/AC7 contributed; AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5/SC1/SC2/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

ROSTER-LEVEL absence (not this child's fault; already tracked on 33d0/59ef/822a/8937 — restated for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the whole roster including this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES the documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 2359 AC4 "The canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to THIS story's own canary; 33d0 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail governing new/future tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 2359 itself for its owned criteria (AC4/SC3 fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked on sibling tickets.
